evals.prompt: I have an Excel file called ItemConf.xlsx with a sheet named ItemConf. The columns are: ID (uint32), Name (string), Price (int32), and Tags (a comma-separated list of strings in one cell). Can you show me what the typerow should look like and what proto would be generated?
evals.expected_output: Correct typerow with map<uint32, Item> for ID, string for Name, int32 for Price, and []string for Tags. Should run tableauc to generate actual proto output rather than hand-writing it.
expectations: Uses map<uint32, Item> as the first column type for a vertical map keyed by ID
Uses []string for the Tags column (incell list)
Suggests running tableauc rather than hand-crafting the proto output
Explains the 4-row header layout (namerow, typerow, noterow, datarow)

evals.prompt: I'm getting error E2016 on my RewardConf sheet. It has columns Reward1ID, Reward1Num, Reward2ID, Reward2Num, Reward3ID, Reward3Num with type [Reward]uint32 on the first column. Some rows have Reward1 and Reward3 filled but Reward2 is empty. The design intent is that some items only give rewards in certain slots.
evals.expected_output: Diagnoses as intentional sparse layout (Scenario B) and recommends adding |{fixed:true} or |{size:3} to the typerow.
expectations: Identifies the issue as an intentional sparse horizontal list
Recommends |{fixed:true} or |{size:3} as the fix
Does NOT suggest filling in missing data as the primary fix since user stated design intent
Explains why fixed/size disables the continuity check

evals.prompt: I want to define a FruitType enum in my workbook with values Apple=1, Orange=2, Grape=3, then use it in my ItemConf sheet's Type column. Walk me through the full setup.
evals.expected_output: Explains MODE_ENUM_TYPE setup in metasheet, enum sheet layout with Name/Alias/Number columns, and how to reference it as enum<.FruitType> in the typerow.
expectations: Explains the enum sheet needs columns: Name, Alias, Number
Mentions setting Mode to MODE_ENUM_TYPE in the @TABLEAU metasheet
Shows the dot-prefix syntax enum<.FruitType> for referencing the enum
Mentions that FRUIT_TYPE_INVALID = 0 is auto-generated

evals.prompt: How do I set up a config.yaml to generate JSON output with pretty-printing, using Asia/Shanghai timezone, and importing types from a common.proto file? I want to use tableauc CLI.
evals.expected_output: Shows a config.yaml with locationName, proto.input.protoFiles, conf.output.formats and conf.output.pretty settings.
expectations: Sets locationName to Asia/Shanghai
Configures proto.input.protoFiles to include common.proto
Sets conf.output.formats to include json
Sets conf.output.pretty to true
References tableauc -c config.yaml for running

evals.prompt: I need to merge data from Zone1.xlsx, Zone2.xlsx, and Zone3.xlsx that all share the same ZoneConf schema into one output. How do I configure the Merger in tableau?
evals.expected_output: Explains Merger configuration in the @TABLEAU metasheet using glob pattern Zone*.xlsx.
expectations: Shows the Merger option in the @TABLEAU metasheet
Uses a glob pattern like Zone*.xlsx for the Merger value
Explains that the main workbook's schema is used as the base
Mentions that sub-workbooks don't need their own @TABLEAU

evals.prompt: My HeroConf sheet has a complex layout: each hero has an ID, Name, and a nested Stats struct with HP, ATK, DEF columns (cross-cell struct). The Stats columns are named StatsHP, StatsATK, StatsDEF. What should the typerow look like, and what proto will this generate?
evals.expected_output: Shows {Stats}int32 for the first struct column, int32 for subsequent columns. Explains cross-cell struct grouping by prefix.
expectations: Uses {Stats}int32 syntax for the first column (StatsHP) to declare a cross-cell struct
Explains that StatsATK and StatsDEF are auto-grouped by the 'Stats' prefix
Mentions that protogen generates a Stats message with hp, atk, def fields
Suggests running tableauc to see the actual proto output

evals.prompt: I want to run just the confgen step with tableauc CLI, outputting text protobuf format instead of JSON. I already have the .proto files generated. What's the exact command and config.yaml setup?
evals.expected_output: Shows tableauc -m conf command with config.yaml setting conf.output.formats to ["txtpb"] for text protobuf output.
expectations: Uses tableauc -m conf to run confgen only
Shows config.yaml with conf.output.formats set to txtpb or text
References the -c flag for config.yaml usage
Does NOT suggest using Go API

evals.prompt: My DropConf sheet uses pipe-separated values instead of commas — for example the Tags column has 'fire|ice|lightning'. But other sheets still use commas. How do I set the separator just for this one column without affecting others?
evals.expected_output: Shows field-level sep property: []string|{sep:"|"} and explains the separator hierarchy.
expectations: Uses field-level |{sep:"|"} on the typerow for that column
Explains the separator hierarchy (field > sheet > global > default)
Does NOT suggest changing the global or sheet-level separator
Mentions that default sep is comma

evals.prompt: I have a column that should be optional — sometimes the column may not exist at all in different versions of the spreadsheet. How do I handle this in tableau so it doesn't error when the column is missing?
evals.expected_output: Shows the |{optional:true} field property and explains the difference between optional fields and FieldPresence.
expectations: Uses |{optional:true} field property syntax
Explains that optional means the column may be absent entirely
Mentions that when set, empty cells produce null in JSON instead of zero values
Distinguishes from FieldPresence which applies to all fields on a sheet

evals.prompt: I want to define a union type for my game's targeting system. There are three target types: PVP (with fields HP and ATK), PVE (with fields Level and Wave), and Story (with field Chapter). How do I set this up using MODE_UNION_TYPE?
evals.expected_output: Shows union sheet layout with Name, Alias, and Field columns. Explains how to reference the union as .Target in other sheets.
expectations: Shows the union sheet layout with Name/Alias/Field1/Field2 columns
Mentions setting Mode to MODE_UNION_TYPE in @TABLEAU metasheet
Explains how to reference the union in data sheets as .Target or enum<.Target.Type>
Shows that the Type column is the discriminator and Field columns hold the data

evals.prompt: I need to use the same Item struct type for both rewards and costs in my sheet, but I want them to have different field names. How do I create two instances of the same struct type with different names?
evals.expected_output: Shows named struct variant syntax: {Item(RewardItem)}int32 and {Item(CostItem)}int32.
expectations: Uses {Item(RewardItem)}int32 syntax for named variant
Shows both reward and cost using the same Item type with different variable names
Explains that column names must use the variable name as prefix (RewardItemID, CostItemID)
Mentions predefined variant syntax {.Item(PredefinedItem)} for external types

evals.prompt: I want to convert my XML configuration files to JSON using tableauc. The XML has nested elements. How do I set up the @TABLEAU metasheet in XML format?
evals.expected_output: Shows XML metasheet as a comment block with <@TABLEAU> tags containing per-sheet configuration elements.
expectations: Shows the XML comment block format: <!--<@TABLEAU>...</@TABLEAU>-->
Demonstrates per-sheet options as XML elements with attributes
Mentions that XML uses element attributes for simple fields and nested elements for complex types
Shows proper XML input format configuration in config.yaml

evals.prompt: I have a YAML config file that I want to convert using tableauc. How does the YAML input format work? I need to understand the three-document structure — metasheet, schema, and data. Show me a complete example with a simple item config.
evals.expected_output: Shows the YAML three-document structure with --- separators, @sheet annotations, @type/@struct for type definitions.
expectations: Shows three YAML documents separated by ---
First doc uses '@sheet': '@TABLEAU' for metasheet
Second doc uses '@sheet': '@SheetName' (with @ prefix) for schema with type definitions
Third doc uses '@sheet': 'SheetName' (without @ prefix) for data
Uses @type and/or @struct annotations for defining types

evals.prompt: My game has environment-specific configs: base settings, then dev/prod/test overlays that override certain values. How do I set up patch/overlay in tableau? I need PATCH_MERGE for most fields but PATCH_REPLACE for one list field.
evals.expected_output: Shows Patch configuration in metasheet with Scatter for overlay files, and field-level |{patch:PATCH_REPLACE} for the list.
expectations: Sets Patch to PATCH_MERGE in the @TABLEAU metasheet
Uses Scatter with a glob pattern to load overlay files
Shows |{patch:PATCH_REPLACE} on the specific list field
Explains the merge behavior: scalars overwrite, messages merge, lists append by default

evals.prompt: I want to set up a keyed list in my EquipConf sheet. Each equipment has a unique ID, and I want an ordered list with key-based access (but not a map). The columns are ID (uint32), SlotID (uint32), and Effect (string). How do I configure this?
evals.expected_output: Shows [Equip]<uint32> syntax for keyed list, explains that keys must be unique (duplicate key = merge), and distinguishes from map.
expectations: Uses [Equip]<uint32> or []<uint32> syntax for keyed list
Explains that keyed list keys must be unique (duplicate key triggers merge, not allowed as separate entries)
Distinguishes keyed list from map (repeated vs map in proto, key uniqueness enforced in both)
Shows the column layout with the key field first

evals.prompt: My spreadsheet has a master-detail layout: the MasterID column has values like 1, (empty), (empty), 2, (empty). The empty rows should inherit the ID from the row above. I think there's an AdjacentKey feature for this. How does it work?
evals.expected_output: Shows AdjacentKey: true in the @TABLEAU metasheet, explains carry-down behavior.
expectations: Sets AdjacentKey to true in the @TABLEAU metasheet
Explains that empty key cells inherit the nearest non-empty value above
Shows an example of the master-detail layout
Mentions this is useful for flat spreadsheets that logically group rows

evals.prompt: I want to create a CSV workbook for tableauc. How does the CSV file naming work? I have three sheets: ItemConf (data), FruitType (enum), and the metasheet. The workbook should be called GameData.
evals.expected_output: Shows CSV naming convention BookName#SheetName.csv and explains CSV workbook = set of files with same BookName prefix.
expectations: Uses the naming pattern GameData#ItemConf.csv, GameData#FruitType.csv, GameData#@TABLEAU.csv
Explains that a CSV workbook is a set of files sharing the same BookName prefix
Mentions the glob pattern BookName#*.csv that tableauc uses to find all sheets
Shows proper metasheet CSV content with Sheet,Mode columns

evals.prompt: I need to generate an Index accessor for my ItemConf sheet so the loader can look up items by Type, and also a composite index by (Type, Rarity). How do I configure this in the @TABLEAU metasheet?
evals.expected_output: Shows Index option syntax in metasheet with single and composite index examples.
expectations: Shows the Index option in the @TABLEAU metasheet
Uses the syntax Type@TypeIndex for single-column index
Uses (Type,Rarity)@TypeRarityIndex for composite index
Mentions OrderedIndex as an alternative for ordered results

evals.prompt: Create Season.xlsx using tableau with two levels of vertical map nesting and a horizontal list inside. Fields: ID uint32 (outer vertical map key), Name string, Phase int32 (inner vertical map key), Item1ID uint32 (horizontal list), Item1Num int32.
evals.expected_output: Creates Season.xlsx with @TABLEAU metasheet and SeasonConf data sheet. Typerow uses map<uint32, Season> for ID, string for Name, map<int32, Phase> for Phase, [Item]uint32 for Item1ID, int32 for Item1Num. Outer map key ID is repeated across rows; Name only filled on first row per ID. Runs tableauc to generate proto with nested Season/Phase/Item messages and JSON with seasonMap containing phaseMap containing itemList.
expectations: Creates @TABLEAU metasheet with SeasonConf sheet entry
Uses map<uint32, Season> as the typerow for the ID column (outer vertical map)
Uses map<int32, Phase> as the typerow for the Phase column (inner vertical map)
Uses [Item]uint32 as the typerow for Item1ID (horizontal list first element)
Repeats the outer map key ID across multiple rows for each inner Phase entry; Name only filled on first row per ID
Runs tableauc to generate actual proto and JSON output
Generated proto has nested Season message containing Phase message containing Item message
Generated JSON has seasonMap -> phaseMap -> itemList structure
